questions.questionText:
A pie chart shows the favourite sports of 120 students.
The angle for Football is 135°.
The angle for Basketball is 90°.
The angle for Tennis is 60°.
The remaining students chose Swimming.

(a) Find the number of students who chose Football.
(b) Find the angle for Swimming.
questions.explanation: Fraction of circle = angle ÷ 360. Number = fraction × total.

questions.context:
The table shows the number of books read by 25 students during one term.

| Number of books | 0 | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 |
|-----------------|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Frequency       | 3 | 5 | 7 | 6 | 3 | 1 |

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Write down the modal number of books.
workingSteps: Highest frequency is 7, corresponding to 2 books → mode = 2

parts.label: b
parts.questionText:
A bar chart is drawn for this data.
Find the height of the bar for 3 books.
workingSteps: Frequency for 3 books = 6, so bar height = 6

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Calculate the angle in a pie chart that would represent students who read 2 books.

parts.label: d
parts.questionText: Calculate the mean number of books read.

markScheme.desc: Σfx attempted: at least 3 correct products

parts.label: e
parts.questionText: Explain why a bar chart is more suitable than a pie chart for comparing the frequencies of each number of books.
parts.correctAnswer: A bar chart shows the actual frequencies so it is easier to compare values directly

markScheme.desc: Bar chart shows exact frequencies / easier to read off individual values / pie chart only shows proportions oe

questions.questionText:
A frequency polygon is drawn for the heights of 40 plants.
The midpoints and frequencies are shown.

| Midpoint (cm) | 10 | 20 | 30 | 40 | 50 |
|--------------|----|----|----|----|----|
| Frequency    | 4  | 10 | 14 | 8  | 4  |

(a) Find the class width used.
(b) Find the total number of plants.
(c) Calculate an estimate of the mean height.
questions.explanation: For grouped data, use midpoints to estimate the mean.
